author: Athanasius of Alexandria
book: On the Incarnation of the Word.

lineage: On the Incarnation of the Word.
Man, unmoved by nature, was to be taught to know God by that sacred Manhood, Whose deity all nature confessed, especially in His Death.

title: Man, unmoved by nature, was to be taught to know God by that sacred Manhood, Whose deity all nature confessed, especially in His Death.

source.title: NPNF2-04. Athanasius: Select Works and Letters

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Man, unmoved by nature, was to be taught to know God by that sacred Manhood, Whose deity all nature confessed, especially in His Death.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: But all this it seemed well for the Saviour to do; that since men had failed to know His Providence, revealed in the Universe, and had failed to perceive His Godhead shewn in creation, they might at any rate from the works of His body recover their sight, and through Him receive an idea of the knowledge of the Father, inferring, as I said before, from particular cases His Providence over the whole.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: For who that saw His power over evil spirits, or who that saw the evil spirits confess that He was their Lord, will hold his mind any longer in doubt whether this be the Son and Wisdom and Power of God?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: For He made even the creation break silence: in that even at His death, marvellous to relate, or rather at His actual trophy over death—the Cross I mean—all creation was confessing that He that was made manifest and suffered in the body was not man merely, but the Son of God and Saviour of all.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: For the sun hid His face, and the earth quaked and the mountains were rent: all men were awed.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Now these things shewed that Christ on the Cross was God, while all creation was His slave, and was witnessing by its fear to its Master’s presence.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Thus, then, God the Word shewed Himself to men by His works.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: But our next step must be to recount and speak of the end of His bodily life and course, and of the nature of the death of His body; especially as this is the sum of our faith, and all men without exception are full of it: so that you may know that no whit the less from this also Christ is known to be God and the Son of God.
